symptoms: Display says hopper temp is high.

tests.error-log: Recurring HOPPER TEMP HIGH alarm, several times a day for two weeks.
tests.temp-probe: Independent probe reads hopper at 3.2 °C — well in spec. Display still claims 9 °C.
tests.continuity-test: Thermistor resistance is way off the curve for its actual temperature. Sensor has drifted.

flavour: The machine wasn't warm. It was lying.
authenticityNote: A drifted thermistor reads high while the product is actually fine, so the controller over-runs the compressor. Looks exactly like refrigeration failure — replacing the compressor is the expensive wrong answer (GDD §2.2).
